symptoms.symptom_name: Intense, escalating scalp pruritus over five days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Delayed hypersensitivity to louse saliva produces the characteristic intense itch; non-specific alone but strongly supportive alongside visualised lice/nits.

symptoms.symptom_name: Live motile lice on the scalp with nits firmly cemented to proximal hair shafts behind the ears and at the nape

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Visualised live lice with firmly cemented nits on the proximal hair shaft is diagnostic of active head lice infestation.
Seborrhoeic scale detaches easily and there are no live insects or cemented nits.
Tinea produces scaling, broken hairs and patchy alopecia, not crawling insects or nits glued to hair shafts.

symptoms.symptom_name: Excoriations with serosanguinous crusting on the scalp and posterior neck (early secondary bacterial colonisation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: secondary bacterial infection / impetiginisation of excoriations
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Serosanguinous crusting over scratched skin indicates early secondary infection; its severity is graded by spread and systemic features, not by the crust alone.
Excoriations are the expected mechanical consequence of intense lice-related itch.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localised, non-spreading sores without thick purulent discharge, and no fever or systemic upset (spread/systemic screen negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: spreading secondary bacterial skin infection (impetigo / cellulitis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised non-advancing crusting in an afebrile, systemically-well child makes a spreading or systemic infection unlikely and supports community management with safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low-grade temperature ~37.1 C on a home thermometer (afebrile)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: spreading secondary bacterial skin infection (impetigo / cellulitis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An essentially normal temperature argues against systemic bacterial infection; it is patient-obtainable and unverified, and supports a benign community course.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: secondary bacterial infection / impetiginisation of excoriations
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reactive nodes are expected with an inflamed, secondarily-infected scalp; this is a clinician-palpated sign sealed from the AI Doctor, which must reason from history instead.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: spreading secondary bacterial skin infection (impetigo / cellulitis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal-range vitals for age fit a systemically-well child; these are clinician-measured and sealed from the AI Doctor, which infers stability from the patient-obtainable data.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Detection wet-combing of conditioned hair with a fine-tooth nit comb to confirm live lice
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms active infestation and is the diagnostic and therapeutic mainstay; can be performed by the caregiver at home and does not require in-person clinician attendance.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bacterial swab (MC&S) of the crusted areas
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only if the secondary infection fails to respond, recurs, or spreads — not required to begin topical treatment for early localised colonisation.
Not indicated in a systemically-well afebrile child; ordering them reflects over-investigation of a clinical diagnosis.
